fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We first show that the dependent product of intrinsic path objects \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen -xx\right \rparen \mathclose {} is a left-cloven over the path object \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}: given an equivalence H : \mathopen {}\left \lparen \approx _{\mathcal {G}}\right \rparen \mathclose {} \approx _{\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \approx _{\mathcal {G}'}\right \rparen \mathclose {} of graphs and a reflexivity datum r\colon \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} x\approx _{\mathcal {G}} x, we define H_*r x :\equiv H\,x\,x\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen rx\right \rparen \mathclose {} ; the unit law holds definitionally.

Therefore, we may form the covariant sum \mathchoice {\textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen Exx\right \rparen \mathclose {} , in which equivalences are given by homotopies of reflexivity data over graph equivalences.

fr:title.text: Path object structure on reflexive graphs

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Path object structure on reflexive graphs

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We may define a path object structure on the type of reflexive graphs with vertices in a given type A, so that an isomorphism from \mathcal {G} to \mathcal {G}' is given by a pair of a pointwise equivalence of types H\colon \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathopen {}\left \lparen x\approx _{\mathcal {G}}y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \cong \mathopen {}\left \lparen x\approx _{\mathcal {G}'}y\right \rparen \mathclose {} that preserves reflexivity data as witnessed by a dependent homotopy I\colon \mathopen {}\left \lparen \lambda {x}\mathpunct {.} H\,x\,x\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {G}}{x}\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} \sim \mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {G}'}.

We first show that the dependent product of intrinsic path objects \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen -xx\right \rparen \mathclose {} is a left-cloven over the path object \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}: given an equivalence H : \mathopen {}\left \lparen \approx _{\mathcal {G}}\right \rparen \mathclose {} \approx _{\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \approx _{\mathcal {G}'}\right \rparen \mathclose {} of graphs and a reflexivity datum r\colon \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} x\approx _{\mathcal {G}} x, we define H_*r x :\equiv H\,x\,x\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen rx\right \rparen \mathclose {} ; the unit law holds definitionally.

Therefore, we may form the covariant sum \mathchoice {\textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen E:\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {Type}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen Exx\right \rparen \mathclose {} , in which equivalences are given by homotopies of reflexivity data over graph equivalences.
